rulings.Case Title: PORTSIDE HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION VS. SF OAKLAND BAY LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, August 19, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONER PORTSIDE HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award. Transferred to be heard in department 501 on 9/5/24 at the same time. That department handles all actions pertaining to real property and licenses per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, August 19, 2024, Line 2. 2 - PETITIONER SHELBY BATES' MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Kiran Bhat's unopposed pro hac vice application is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE C RODRIGUEZ JR VS. HEALTHRIGHT 360 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Spoliation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Monday, August 19, 2024, Line 4. PLAINTIFF JOSE RODRIGUEZ JR's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Spoliation. Plaintiff's "motion for reconsideration of order denying plaintiff's motion to compel and motion for spoliation" is denied. A reconsideration motion must be based on "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) Plaintiff says the purported new facts were in Steve Garcia's June 6, 2024 deposition. However, Garcia was discussed in the May 13 initial hearing on plaintiff's two discovery motions, but then plaintiff did not depose Garcia for another 24 days. Moreover, the court's order on the two motions was not filed until June 10, so plaintiff still had four days to present his "new facts" to the court after the June 6 deposition (the ex parte calendar was available). In short, plaintiff shows no valid reason for failing to present his facts earlier. No sanctions are awarded. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
